title: S3/G3: Geometric fixed points of O-slice cells and closure under the filtration

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Necessity direction, computational core.** To prove that membership in $\tau^{\aO}_{\ge n}$ implies the geometric-fixed-point connectivity bound, compute $\Phi^H$ on the generators and show the bound is preserved.

**To prove.**
1. Double-coset/Weyl formula for geometric fixed points of an induction: $\Phi^H(G_+\wedge_K Y)\simeq \bigvee_{[g]\in (H\backslash G/K)_{\mathrm{rel}}}\Phi^{H\cap{}^gK}(c_g^* Y)$, with the correct index set.
2. $\Phi^K S^{m\rho_K}\simeq S^{m}$, more generally $\Phi^K S^{V}\simeq S^{\dim V^K}$.
3. Deduce the connectivity of $\Phi^H(G_+\wedge_K S^{m\rho_K})$ and verify it equals the claimed $f_{\aO}(n,H)$ bound on generators.
4. Show the connectivity bound is closed under the colimits and extensions defining $\tau^{\aO}_{\ge n}$, using exactness/colimit-preservation of $\Phi^H$ and closure of connectivity in $\mathrm{Sp}$.

Reference: HHR 2016 §B (geometric fixed points), Hill primer.
